name: Ya Shi Xiang
region: Ping Keng Tou, Phoenix Mountains, Chaozhou, Guangdong
terroir: High mountain village, mineral-rich soil, cool mountain climate
flavor_profile: Despite the colorful name, Ya Shi Xiang is one of the most elegant Dan Congs. Lightly oxidized with mostly green leaves, it produces a bright orange-yellow liquor loaded with floral and honey aromatics. Longan sweetness builds through multiple steeps with mountain minerality underneath. Exceptional endurance — holds flavor deep into the session.

brewing.rinse_hint: One quick rinse. Ya Shi Xiang is lightly oxidized — the leaves open fast.
brewing.tips: Ya Shi Xiang is more forgiving than most Dan Congs but still rewards quick pours. Its lighter oxidation means the aromatics are in the first few steeps — slow down only after steep five or so.
